397 |     private suspend fun checkRules() {

424 |         val hour = cal.get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
425 |         val minute = cal.get(java.util.Calendar.MINUTE)
426 |         val currentMs = (hour * 60 + minute) * 60 * 1000L + cal.get(java.util.Calendar.SECOND) * 1000L
427 |         val target11PM = 23 * 60 * 60 * 1000L
428 |         
429 |         if (hour >= 23 || hour < 5) {
430 |              hideBanner()
431 |              triggerBreak("SLEEP_MODE", 0)
432 |              return
433 |         }
434 |         
435 |         val msToSleep = target11PM - currentMs
436 |         if (msToSleep in 1..15 * 60 * 1000) {
437 |              handleSleepWarnings(msToSleep)

439 | 
440 |         // --- 2. USAGE LOGIC ---
441 |         
442 |         // Priority A: 3 HOURS (15 Min Break)
443 |         if (nextLongBreak - usage <= 5 * 60 * 1000) {
444 |              handleLongBreakWarnings(nextLongBreak - usage)
445 |              return // Priority
446 |         }
447 | 
448 |         // Priority B: 1 HOUR (5 Min Break)
449 |         if (nextMediumBreak - usage <= 60 * 1000) {
450 |              handleMediumBreakWarnings(nextMediumBreak - usage)
451 |              return
452 |         }
453 | 
454 |         // Priority C: 30 MINS (30 Sec Break)
455 |         if (nextShortBreak - usage <= 10 * 1000) {
456 |              handleShortBreakCountdown(nextShortBreak - usage)
457 |         }
458 |     }

460 |     private suspend fun handleSleepWarnings(remaining: Long) {
461 |         if (remaining <= 10_000) {
462 |              runCountdown("Sleep Mode", remaining)
463 |              triggerBreak("SLEEP_MODE", 0)
464 |              return
465 |         }
466 |         if (remaining <= 60_000) {

470 |             }
471 |             return
472 |         }

477 |             }
478 |             return
479 |         }

483 |                 sleepWarn15 = true
484 |             }
485 |         }
486 |     }

492 |              resetCounters(true)

514 |              nextMediumBreak += TimeManager.INTERVAL_60_MIN

516 |              medWarn1 = false

private fun startForegroundService() {
        val channelId = "eyecare_min_channel"
        val nm = getSystemService(android.app.NotificationManager::class.java)
        
        if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
            val chan = android.app.NotificationChannel(
                channelId, 
                "Monitor", 
                android.app.NotificationManager.IMPORTANCE_MIN
            )
            chan.setShowBadge(false)
            nm.createNotificationChannel(chan)
        }

        val notif = androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.Builder(this, channelId)
            .setContentTitle("Eyecare")
            .setContentText("Active")
            .setSmallIcon(android.R.drawable.ic_menu_view)
            .setPriority(androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.PRIORITY_MIN)
            .setCategory(androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.CATEGORY_SERVICE)
            .setOngoing(true)
            .build()
        
        startForeground(2024, notif)
    }

    override fun onServiceConnected() {
        super.onServiceConnected()
        startForegroundService()
        StatsManager.init(this)
        TimeManager.init(this)
        windowManager = getSystemService(WindowManager::class.java)
        TimeManager.sessionStart = System.currentTimeMillis()

private fun startForegroundService() {
        val channelId = "eyecare_min_channel"
        val nm = getSystemService(android.app.NotificationManager::class.java)
        
        if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
            val chan = android.app.NotificationChannel(
                channelId, 
                "Monitor", 
                android.app.NotificationManager.IMPORTANCE_MIN
            )
            chan.setShowBadge(false)
            nm.createNotificationChannel(chan)
        }

        val notif = androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.Builder(this, channelId)
            .setContentTitle("Eyecare")
            .setContentText("Active")
            .setSmallIcon(android.R.drawable.ic_menu_view)
            .setPriority(androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.PRIORITY_MIN)
            .setCategory(androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.CATEGORY_SERVICE)
            .setOngoing(true)
            .build()

        startForegroundService()

333 |             }

340 |         val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
341 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
342 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)

347 |     // HELPER: Recursive scan for the Dialog Trap
348 |     private fun recursiveScan(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?, sb: StringBuilder) {
349 |         if (node == null) return
350 |         if (node.text != null) sb.append(node.text).append(" ")
351 |         if (node.contentDescription != null) sb.append(node.contentDescription).append(" ")
352 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
353 |             recursiveScan(node.getChild(i), sb)
354 |         }

359 |     private fun startMonitoring() {
360 |         scope.launch {
361 |             while (isActive) {
362 |                 // SKIP CHECKS IF UNLOCKED
363 |                 if (LockManager.isUnlocked(applicationContext)) {
364 |                     delay(2000)
365 |                     continue
366 |                 }
367 | 
368 |                 // FIX: GRACE PERIOD (2000ms flicker)
369 |                 if (System.currentTimeMillis() - lastSettingsInteraction < 2000) {
370 |                     delay(500)
371 |                     continue
372 |                 }
373 | 
374 |                 // 1. SELF-HEALING: Check if Overlay Permission was revoked
375 |                 if (!Settings.canDrawOverlays(applicationContext)) {
376 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_MANAGE_OVERLAY_PERMISSION)
377 |                     i.data = Uri.parse("package:$packageName")
378 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
379 |                     startActivity(i)
380 |                 }
381 |                 // 2. ENFORCE AUTO TIME (Required for Nuke Timer)
382 |                 else if ((Settings.Global.getInt(contentResolver, Settings.Global.AUTO_TIME, 0) != 1 ||
383 |                           Settings.Global.getInt(contentResolver, Settings.Global.AUTO_TIME_ZONE, 0) != 1)) {
384 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_DATE_SETTINGS)
385 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
386 |                     startActivity(i)
387 |                 }
388 |                 // 3. ENFORCE ENGLISH LANGUAGE (Required for Text Scanners)
389 |                 else if (java.util.Locale.getDefault().language != "en") {
390 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_LOCALE_SETTINGS)
391 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
392 |                     startActivity(i)
393 |                 }
394 |                 // 4. CHECK DNS
395 |                 else if (!DnsManager.isSecure(applicationContext)) {
396 |                     try {
397 |                         val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
398 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
399 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_SINGLE_TOP)
400 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
401 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
402 |                         startActivity(i)
403 |                     } catch (e: Exception) { e.printStackTrace() }
404 |                 }
405 | 
406 |                 // 5. CLOUD SYNC (Hourly)
407 |                 // Keeps the bad words database updated
408 |                 val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
409 |                 if (now - lastCloudSync > 60 * 60 * 1000) { // 1 Hour
410 |                     lastCloudSync = now
411 |                     scope.launch {
412 |                         CloudLogger.syncToCloud(applicationContext)
413 |                     }
414 |                 }
415 | 
416 |                 // METRICS: Update Performance Stats
417 |                 StatsManager.update(applicationContext)
418 | 
419 |                 // OPTIMIZATION: Smart Sleep to save battery
420 |                 val pm = getSystemService(android.os.PowerManager::class.java)
421 |                 if (pm.isInteractive) {
422 |                     delay(2000)
423 |                 } else {
424 |                     delay(10000)
425 |                 }
426 |             }
427 |         }
428 |     }

436 |                        pkg == "com.android.chrome" ||

477 |                 // Creator & Membership
478 |                 "onlyfans.com", "fansly.com", "patreon.com", "subscribestar.com", "fanbox.cc", "unifans.io", "buymeacoffee.com", "ko-fi.com",
479 |                 // Video & Streaming

490 |                     val resId = node.viewIdResourceName?.lowercase() ?: ""
491 |                     
492 |                     // FIX: Broadened IDs to catch Firefox/Opera/Samsung 'Read-Only' URL bars
493 |                     val isUrlBar = node.isEditable || 
494 |                                    resId.contains("url") || 
495 |                                    resId.contains("address") ||

501 |                                    resId.contains("input") ||

505 |                                    resId.contains("search_text") ||
506 |                                    resId.contains("edit_text") ||
507 |                                    resId.contains("query")

509 |                     // However, if we are unsure, we err on the side of caution if the node is NOT a web content view.
510 |                     val isWebContent = resId.contains("content") || node.className == "android.webkit.WebView"
511 | 
512 |                     // HYBRID FALLBACK:
513 |                     // 1. If it's a URL bar (ID match), we scan it.
514 |                     // 2. If it's NOT a URL bar, we check if it's Web Content.
515 |                     //    - If it IS Web Content (and not a URL bar), we skip it (False Positive Protection).
516 |                     //    - If it is NOT Web Content (it's UI, like Firefox toolbar), we scan it.
517 |                     if (!isUrlBar && isWebContent) continue
518 | 
519 |                     
520 |                     // 2. TEXT MATCHING
521 |                     val rawText = (node.text?.toString() ?: "") + " " + (node.contentDescription?.toString() ?: "")

523 |                     val index = lowerText.indexOf(site)
524 | 
525 |                     if (index != -1) {
526 |                         // STRICT CHECK: char before must NOT be letter/digit
527 |                         val charBefore = if (index > 0) lowerText[index - 1] else ' '
528 | 
529 |                         if (!charBefore.isLetterOrDigit()) {

552 |             val visibleTabs = setOf("chats", "channels", "apps", "posts")
553 |             val hiddenTabs = setOf("media", "downloads", "links", "files", "music", "voice")

182 | 
183 |         // 2. BROWSER & VPN GUARD (Active ONLY when Unlocked)
184 |         if (LockManager.isUnlocked(applicationContext)) {
185 |             if (LockManager.isBlacklistedBrowser(applicationContext, pkg)) {
186 |                 // Launch Lockdown UI with Browser Block message
187 |                 val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
188 |                 i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "BROWSER")
189 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
190 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_SINGLE_TOP)
191 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
192 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
193 |                 startActivity(i)
194 |             }
195 |         }
196 | 
197 |         // 3. NUKE CHECK: If Nuke Protocol is active, we STOP here.
198 |         if (NukeManager.isProtectionDisabled(applicationContext)) {
199 |             return
200 |         }
201 | 
202 |         // 4. TELEGRAM GUARD (Ban Enforcement Only)
203 |         // We removed the instant-ban scanner from here. The Strike System in scanForViolations() handles the rest.
204 |         if (pkg.contains("telegram") || pkg.contains("challegram")) {
205 |             if (LockManager.isTelegramBanned(applicationContext)) {
206 |                 val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
207 |                 i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "TELEGRAM_SUSPENDED")
208 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
209 |                 startActivity(i)
210 |                 
211 |                 // Block interaction but don't force Home, let the Overlay sit there.
212 |                 performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
213 |                 return
214 |             }
215 |         }
216 | 
217 |         // 5. OMNI-BROWSER GUARD: REMOVED
218 |         // We now rely solely on scanForViolations() (Polling & TextChange)
219 |         // because it implements the Strict Boundary Check to prevent false positives.
220 | 
221 |         // 6. SETTINGS GUARD (Always Active - Locked OR Unlocked)
222 |         if (pkg.contains("settings") || pkg.contains("accessibility") || pkg.contains("packageinstaller")) {
223 |             // OPTIMIZED DETECTION: Native Class Detection
224 |             val cls = event.className?.toString()?.lowercase() ?: ""
225 |             
226 |             // DETECT APP INFO: Multiple Triggers (Header, Class, or Bottom Buttons)
227 |             val rootNode = rootInActiveWindow
228 |             val headerText = rootNode?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("App info")
229 |             val hasAppInfoHeader = headerText != null && headerText.isNotEmpty()
230 | 
231 |             // FIX: If header is scrolled away, check for persistent bottom buttons.
232 |             // "Uninstall" and "Force stop" are strong indicators of the App Info page.
233 |             val hasUninstall = rootNode?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Uninstall")?.isNotEmpty() == true
234 |             val hasForceStop = rootNode?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Force stop")?.isNotEmpty() == true
235 | 
236 |             val isAppInfoPage = cls.contains("installedappdetails") || 
237 |                                 cls.contains("appmanagement") || 
238 |                                 hasAppInfoHeader || 
239 |                                 (hasUninstall && hasForceStop)
240 |             
241 |             var confirmedDanger = false
242 |             
243 |             // MULTI-WINDOW DEFENSE: Content Scanning
244 |             val allWindows = this.windows
245 |             if (!allWindows.isEmpty()) {
246 |                 for (window in allWindows) {
247 |                     val root = window.root ?: continue
248 |                     
249 |                     // GLOBAL SCAN: Locate identifying strings first
250 |                     // We look for the App Name "Guardian" to detect our own App Info page
251 |                     val hasDnsGuard = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Guardian")
252 |                     
253 |                     // A. ACCESSIBILITY TRAP (Targeted)
254 |                     val trap1 = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Monitors system settings")
255 |                     val trap2 = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("enforce Private DNS")
256 |                     
257 |                     if (trap1.isNotEmpty() || trap2.isNotEmpty()) {
258 |                         confirmedDanger = true
259 |                         performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
260 |                         startTripwire()
261 |                         break
262 |                     }
263 | 
264 |                     // B. DEVICE ADMIN TRAP
265 |                     val hasDeactivate = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Deactivate")
266 |                     if (hasDeactivate.isNotEmpty() && hasDnsGuard.isNotEmpty()) {
267 |                          confirmedDanger = true
268 |                          performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
269 |                          startTripwire()
270 |                          break
271 |                     }
272 | 
273 |                     // C. SELF-DEFENSE (App Info & Storage Guard)
274 |                     if (isAppInfoPage) {
275 |                         val nodePkg = root.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
276 |                         val isSettingsWindow = nodePkg.contains("settings") || nodePkg.contains("packageinstaller")
277 | 
278 |                         if (isSettingsWindow && hasDnsGuard.isNotEmpty()) {
279 |                             confirmedDanger = true
280 |                             performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
281 |                             startTripwire()
282 |                             break
283 |                         }
284 |                     }
285 |                 }
286 |             }
287 |             
288 |             // VERDICT: THREAT CONFIRMED
289 |             if (confirmedDanger) {
290 |                 // CASE 1: THREAT CONFIRMED -> LOCKDOWN ACTIVITY ONLY (No Back Press)
291 |                 DebugLogger.log("BLOCK", "Threat Confirmed! Launching Tripwire.")
292 |                 shieldJob?.cancel()
293 |                 startTripwire()
294 |             } else if (isAppInfoPage) {
295 |                 DebugLogger.log("Check", "AppInfoPage Detected. VerifiedSession: $verifiedSafeAppInfoSession")
296 |                 // CASE 2: APP INFO PAGE (Ambiguous)
297 |                 shieldJob?.cancel()
298 |                 
299 |                 // VERIFICATION: Look for "Notifications" anchor.
300 |                 val hasAnchor = rootInActiveWindow?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Notifications")?.isNotEmpty() == true
301 |                 DebugLogger.log("Check", "Anchor 'Notifications' found: $hasAnchor")
302 | 
303 |                 if (verifiedSafeAppInfoSession) {
304 |                     DebugLogger.log("Allow", "Session previously verified. Scrolling allowed.")
305 |                     setShield(false)
306 |                 } else if (hasAnchor) {
307 |                     DebugLogger.log("Allow", "Anchor found. Marking session verified.")
308 |                     verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = true
309 |                     setShield(false)
310 |                 } else {
311 |                     DebugLogger.log("BLOCK", "KICK OUT! Anchor missing and session not verified.")
312 |                     // CASE 3: HIDDEN/SCROLLED AWAY -> KICK OUT
313 |                     scope.launch {
314 |                         repeat(4) {
315 |                             performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
316 |                             delay(100)
317 |                         }
318 |                     }
319 |                 }
320 |             } else {
321 |                 // CASE 4: GENERAL SETTINGS / MENU
322 |                 shieldJob?.cancel()
323 |                 
324 |                 // SAFE RESET: Only reset if we POSITIVELY see the main Settings header.
325 |                 // If we simply don't match 'App Info', we might just be scrolling and missed the detection.
326 |                 val root = rootInActiveWindow
327 |                 val hasSettingsHeader = root?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Settings")?.isNotEmpty() == true
328 |                 val hasSearchHeader = root?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Search settings")?.isNotEmpty() == true
329 | 
330 |                 if (hasSettingsHeader || hasSearchHeader) {
331 |                     if (verifiedSafeAppInfoSession) {
332 |                          DebugLogger.log("Reset", "Back in Main Settings. Session Invalidated.")
333 |                          verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = false
334 |                     }
335 |                 }
336 | 
337 |                 shieldJob = scope.launch {
338 |                     delay(1000)
339 |                     withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
340 |                         if (isShieldActive) setShield(false)
341 |                     }
342 |                 }
343 |             }
344 |         } else {
345 |             // Not in settings? Shield down.
346 |             setShield(false)
347 |         }
348 |     }

350 |     // SHIELD LOGIC (High Performance Mode)
351 |     private fun setShield(active: Boolean) {
352 |         if (active == isShieldActive) return
353 |         
354 |         try {
355 |             if (shieldView == null) {
356 |                 shieldView = android.view.View(this).apply {
357 |                     setBackgroundColor(0x00000000) // Transparent
358 |                     isClickable = true
359 |                     isFocusable = true
360 |                 }
361 |             }
362 | 
363 |             val params = android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams(
364 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
365 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
366 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.TYPE_ACCESSIBILITY_OVERLAY,
367 |                 // RESTORED FLAG_NOT_FOCUSABLE to prevent Keyboard flickering in Search
368 |                 // The shield still blocks touches because it is fullscreen.
369 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_NOT_FOCUSABLE or
370 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_LAYOUT_IN_SCREEN or
371 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_WATCH_OUTSIDE_TOUCH,
372 |                 android.graphics.PixelFormat.TRANSLUCENT
373 |             )
374 | 
375 |             if (active) {
376 |                 if (shieldView?.parent == null) {
377 |                     windowManager?.addView(shieldView, params)
378 |                 } else {
379 |                     shieldView?.visibility = android.view.View.VISIBLE
380 |                     windowManager?.updateViewLayout(shieldView, params)
381 |                 }
382 |                 isShieldActive = true
383 |             } else {
384 |                 // Optimization: Don't remove view, just HIDE it. 
385 |                 // This eliminates the 'addView' lag for the next trigger.
386 |                 if (shieldView?.parent != null) {
387 |                     shieldView?.visibility = android.view.View.GONE
388 |                 }
389 |                 isShieldActive = false
390 |             }
391 |         } catch (e: Exception) {
392 |             e.printStackTrace()
393 |         }
394 |     }

399 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
400 |         i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "SECURITY_TRIPWIRE")
401 |         startActivity(i)

404 |     // HELPER: Recursive scan for the Dialog Trap
405 |     private fun recursiveScan(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?, sb: StringBuilder) {
406 |         if (node == null) return
407 |         if (node.text != null) sb.append(node.text).append(" ")
408 |         if (node.contentDescription != null) sb.append(node.contentDescription).append(" ")
409 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
410 |             recursiveScan(node.getChild(i), sb)
411 |         }
412 |     }

417 |         scope.launch {
418 |             while (isActive) {
419 |                 // 0. AUTO-LOCK WATCHDOG
420 |                 NukeManager.checkAutoReEnable(applicationContext)
421 |                 NukeManager.checkNotifications(applicationContext)
422 | 
423 |                 // SKIP CHECKS IF UNLOCKED
424 |                 if (LockManager.isUnlocked(applicationContext)) {
425 |                     delay(2000)

427 |                 }
428 | 
429 |                 // 3. NUKE CHECK: If Nuke Protocol is active, we STOP here.
430 |                 if (NukeManager.isProtectionDisabled(applicationContext)) {
431 |                     delay(5000) // Lower frequency check while Nuked
432 |                     continue
433 |                 }
434 | 
435 |                 // FIX: GRACE PERIOD (2000ms flicker)
436 |                 if (System.currentTimeMillis() - lastSettingsInteraction < 2000) {
437 |                     delay(500)
438 |                     continue
439 |                 }
440 | 
441 |                 // 1. SELF-HEALING: Check if Overlay Permission was revoked

448 |                 // 2. ENFORCE AUTO TIME (Required for Nuke Timer)
449 |                 else if ((Settings.Global.getInt(contentResolver, Settings.Global.AUTO_TIME, 0) != 1 ||
450 |                           Settings.Global.getInt(contentResolver, Settings.Global.AUTO_TIME_ZONE, 0) != 1)) {
451 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_DATE_SETTINGS)
452 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
453 |                     startActivity(i)
454 |                 }
455 |                 // 3. ENFORCE ENGLISH LANGUAGE (Required for Text Scanners)
456 |                 else if (java.util.Locale.getDefault().language != "en") {
457 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_LOCALE_SETTINGS)
458 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
459 |                     startActivity(i)
460 |                 }
461 |                 // 4. CHECK DNS
462 |                 else if (!DnsManager.isSecure(applicationContext)) {
463 |                     try {
464 |                         val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
465 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
466 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_SINGLE_TOP)
467 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
468 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
469 |                         startActivity(i)
470 |                     } catch (e: Exception) { e.printStackTrace() }
471 |                 }
472 | 
473 |                 // 5. CLOUD SYNC (Hourly)
474 |                 // Keeps the bad words database updated
475 |                 val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
476 |                 if (now - lastCloudSync > 60 * 60 * 1000) { // 1 Hour
477 |                     lastCloudSync = now
478 |                     scope.launch {
479 |                         CloudLogger.syncToCloud(applicationContext)
480 |                     }
481 |                 }
482 | 
483 |                 // METRICS: Update Performance Stats
484 |                 StatsManager.update(applicationContext)
485 | 
486 |                 // OPTIMIZATION: Smart Sleep to save battery
487 |                 val pm = getSystemService(android.os.PowerManager::class.java)
488 |                 if (pm.isInteractive) {
489 |                     delay(2000)
490 |                 } else {
491 |                     delay(10000)
492 |                 }
493 |             }
494 |         }

503 |                        pkg == "com.android.chrome" ||

519 |     private fun scanForViolations(isBrowser: Boolean, isTelegram: Boolean) {
520 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
521 | 
522 |         // 1. BROWSER LOGIC (Strict Domain Matching)
523 |         if (isBrowser) {
524 |             // DEBUG SPY: Independent scan to capture URL bar IDs.
525 |             // Triggers if you type "google" or "http" to help you find the ID.
526 |             val spyKeywords = listOf("google", "http")
527 |             for (key in spyKeywords) {
528 |                 val spies = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText(key)
529 |                 for (node in spies) {
530 |                     val resId = node.viewIdResourceName?.lowercase() ?: "null"
531 |                     DebugLogger.log("SPY", "Pkg: $activePackage | ID: $resId | Text: ${node.text}")
532 |                 }
533 |             }
534 | 
535 |             val blacklist = listOf(
536 |                 // Social & Microblogging
537 |                 "bsky.app", "twitter.com", "x.com", "reddit.com", "tumblr.com", "threads.net", "plurk.com", "hive.social",
538 |                 // Fediverse
539 |                 "mastodon.social", "pawoo.net", "misskey.io", "pleroma.site", "lemmy.world", "truthsocial.com", "gab.com",
540 |                 // Community & Messaging
541 |                 "web.telegram.org", "t.me", "telegram.org", "discord.com", "kik.com", "snapchat.com", "slack.com",
542 |                 // Art & Creative
543 |                 "pixiv.net", "deviantart.com", "newgrounds.com", "artstation.com", "furaffinity.net", "hentai-foundry.com", "gelbooru.com", "danbooru.donmai.us",
544 |                 // Creator & Membership
545 |                 "onlyfans.com", "fansly.com", "patreon.com", "subscribestar.com", "fanbox.cc", "unifans.io", "buymeacoffee.com", "ko-fi.com",
546 |                 // Video & Streaming
547 |                 "kick.com", "bitchute.com", "rumble.com", "vimeo.com", "dailymotion.com", "dlive.tv", "picarto.tv",
548 |                 // Specialized & Existing
549 |                 "fetlife.com", "badoo.com", "tinder.com", "yubo.live", "instagram.com", "tiktok.com", "pornhub", "xnxx"
550 |             )
551 |             
552 |             for (site in blacklist) {
553 |                 val candidates = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText(site)
554 |                 for (node in candidates) {
555 |                     // 1. CONTEXT CHECK: Is this the URL Bar?
556 |                     // We check if the node is editable (typing) OR if its ID indicates it's an address bar.
557 |                     val resId = node.viewIdResourceName?.lowercase() ?: ""
558 |                     
559 |                     // FIX: Broadened IDs to catch Firefox/Opera/Samsung 'Read-Only' URL bars
560 |                     val isUrlBar = node.isEditable || 
561 |                                    resId.contains("url") || 
562 |                                    resId.contains("address") || 
563 |                                    resId.contains("omnibox") || 
564 |                                    resId.contains("search_box") || 
565 |                                    resId.contains("location") ||
566 |                                    resId.contains("toolbar") ||
567 |                                    resId.contains("title") ||
568 |                                    resId.contains("input") ||
569 |                                    resId.contains("bar") ||
570 |                                    // COMPREHENSIVE LIST (Firefox, Bing, Samsung, etc)
571 |                                    resId.contains("mozac") ||
572 |                                    resId.contains("search_text") ||
573 |                                    resId.contains("edit_text") ||
574 |                                    resId.contains("query")
575 |                     // If it's just static text on a page (e.g. a Google Search Result description), ignore it.
576 |                     // However, if we are unsure, we err on the side of caution if the node is NOT a web content view.
577 |                     val isWebContent = resId.contains("content") || node.className == "android.webkit.WebView"
578 | 
579 |                     // HYBRID FALLBACK:
580 |                     // 1. If it's a URL bar (ID match), we scan it.
581 |                     // 2. If it's NOT a URL bar, we check if it's Web Content.
582 |                     //    - If it IS Web Content (and not a URL bar), we skip it (False Positive Protection).
583 |                     //    - If it is NOT Web Content (it's UI, like Firefox toolbar), we scan it.
584 |                     if (!isUrlBar && isWebContent) continue
585 | 
586 |                     
587 |                     // 2. TEXT MATCHING
588 |                     val rawText = (node.text?.toString() ?: "") + " " + (node.contentDescription?.toString() ?: "")
589 |                     val lowerText = rawText.lowercase()
590 |                     val index = lowerText.indexOf(site)
591 | 
592 |                     if (index != -1) {
593 |                         // STRICT CHECK: char before must NOT be letter/digit
594 |                         val charBefore = if (index > 0) lowerText[index - 1] else ' '
595 | 
596 |                         if (!charBefore.isLetterOrDigit()) {
597 |                             // CHECK: Is this a Non-Standard App?
598 |                             if (LockManager.isNonStandardApp(applicationContext, activePackage)) {
599 |                                 // IMMEDIATE 30 MIN BLOCK
600 |                                 LockManager.banNonStandardApp(applicationContext)
601 |                                 val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
602 |                                 i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "ROGUE_VIOLATION")
603 |                                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
604 |                                 startActivity(i)
605 |                             } else {
606 |                                 // STANDARD STRIKE SYSTEM
607 |                                 handleBrowserStrike()
608 |                             }
609 |                             return
610 |                         }
611 |                 }
612 |             }
613 |         }
614 |     }
615 | 
616 |         // 2. TELEGRAM LOGIC (Fingerprint Scan)
617 |         if (isTelegram) {
618 |             // FINGERPRINT: The specific UI structure of the Search Screen
619 |             val visibleTabs = setOf("chats", "channels", "apps", "posts")
620 |             val hiddenTabs = setOf("media", "downloads", "links", "files", "music", "voice")
621 |             val allTabs = visibleTabs + hiddenTabs + "global search"
622 | 
623 |             // A. EXTRACT ALL TEXT
624 |             val screenContent = mutableListOf<String>()
625 |             fun extract(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?) {
626 |                 if (node == null) return
627 |                 if (!node.text.isNullOrBlank()) screenContent.add(node.text.toString())
628 |                 if (!node.contentDescription.isNullOrBlank()) screenContent.add(node.contentDescription.toString())
629 |                 for (i in 0 until node.childCount) extract(node.getChild(i))
630 |             }
631 |             extract(root)
632 | 
633 |             // B. CALCULATE CONFIDENCE & FILTER
634 |             var matchCount = 0
635 |             val contentToCheck = StringBuilder()
636 | 
637 |             for (text in screenContent) {
638 |                 val lower = text.trim().lowercase()
639 |                 if (allTabs.contains(lower)) {
640 |                     // It matches our fingerprint -> Increase Confidence
641 |                     matchCount++
642 |                 } else {
643 |                     // It is NOT a UI tab -> This is content we must check (e.g. what you typed)
644 |                     contentToCheck.append(text).append(" ")
645 |                 }
646 |             }
647 | 
648 |             // C. MATHEMATICAL PROOF (Threshold)
649 |             // We require at least 3 UI elements to match before we assume this is the Search Screen.
650 |             // This prevents false positives (e.g., chatting about "Channels" in a group).
651 |             if (matchCount >= 3) {
652 |                 val finalContent = contentToCheck.toString()
653 | 
654 |                 if (!WordBank.isSafe(applicationContext, finalContent)) {
655 |                    scope.launch { CloudLogger.logViolation(applicationContext, finalContent) }
656 |                    handleTelegramStrike()
657 |                 }
658 |             }
659 |         }
660 |     }

662 |     private fun handleBrowserStrike() {
663 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
664 |         if (now - lastBrowserAction < 1000) return // Debounce (1s cooldown)
665 |         lastBrowserAction = now
666 | 
667 |         browserStrikes.add(now)
668 |         browserStrikes.removeAll { it < now - 10000 }
669 | 
670 |         if (browserStrikes.size >= 4) {
671 |              // TRIGGER BLOCK & BAN
672 |              LockManager.banBrowser(applicationContext)
673 |              val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
674 |              i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "BROWSER_VIOLATION")
675 |              i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
676 |              startActivity(i)
677 |         } else {
678 |              // WARNING STRIKE
679 |              performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
680 |         }
681 |     }

683 |     private fun handleTelegramStrike() {
684 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
685 |         if (now - lastTelegramAction < 1000) return // Debounce (1s cooldown)
686 |         lastTelegramAction = now
687 | 
688 |         telegramStrikes.add(now)
689 |         telegramStrikes.removeAll { it < now - 10000 }
690 | 
691 |         if (telegramStrikes.size >= 3) {
692 |              // 3rd Strike: Activate Ban & Overlay
693 |              LockManager.banTelegram(applicationContext)
694 |              val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
695 |              i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "TELEGRAM_SUSPENDED")
696 |              i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
697 |              startActivity(i)
698 |         } else {
699 |              // 1st & 2nd Strike: Double Back Tap (Clear Search -> Close Keyboard)
700 |              scope.launch {
701 |                  performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
702 |                  delay(250) // Short delay to allow UI to react
703 |                  performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
704 |              }
705 |         }
706 |     }

data.analysis:
The log indicates a **build failure** during the Kotlin compilation phase, specifically within the `:app:compileDebugKotlin` task.

Here is the specific error information:

* **Specific Error:** Compilation errors related to conflicting declarations and overload resolution ambiguity in `EyeCareService.kt`, and unresolved references/incorrect usage of suspend functions in `LockdownActivity.kt`.
* **File/Line:**
    * `file:///home/runner/work/Eyecare/Eyecare/app/src/main/java/com/eyecare/guard/EyeCareService.kt`: Lines 53, 56, 121, 148, and 157 (all related to `verifiedSafeAppInfoSession`).
    * `file:///home/runner/work/Eyecare/Eyecare/app/src/main/java/com/eyecare/guard/LockdownActivity.kt`: Line 38 (`Unresolved reference: LaunchedEffect`) and Line 40 (`Suspend function 'delay' should be called only from a coroutine...`).
* **Brief Summary:** The Gradle build failed on task `:app:compileDebugKotlin` due to **Kotlin compilation errors** in `EyeCareService.kt` (conflicting variable declarations) and `LockdownActivity.kt` (improper use of coroutine functions like `LaunchedEffect` and `delay`).

Additionally, there were **caching errors** during the post-job cleanup, where the system failed to save several parts of the Gradle cache, indicating temporary service unavailability on the runner infrastructure at that time. However, the primary reason for the job failure is the compilation error.

ops.text: Fix duplicate declarations in EyeCareService and resolve coroutine scope errors in LockdownActivity

Here is a summary of the specific error: * **Specific Error:** `e: file:///home/runner/work/Eyecare/Eyecare/app/src/main/java/com/eyecare/guard/EyeCareService.kt:115:13 Unresolved reference: verifiedSafeAppInfoSession` (Similar errors appear on lines 142 and 151 of the same file). * **File and Line:** `app/src/main/java/com/eyecare/guard/EyeCareService.kt` at lines 115, 142, and 151. * **Brief Summary:** The Kotlin compiler could not find a reference to `verifiedSafeAppInfoSession` in the `EyeCareService.kt` file, indicating that this variable, object, or function is either undefined, has not been imported, or is not in scope. **Secondary Observation (Infrastructure/Environment Issue):** There were numerous warnings about **failed cache saves** during post-job cleanup, referencing errors like: `Error:

Our services aren't available right now

We're working to restore all services as soon as possible. Please check back soon.

129 |              val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
130 |              i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "BROWSER_VIOLATION")
131 |              i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
132 |              startActivity(i)
133 |              performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
134 |              return
135 |         }
136 | 
137 |         // REAL-TIME TRIGGER: Run check immediately on text/content changes
138 |         // This acts as the "Keylogger" to catch typing instantly.
139 |         if (event.eventType == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED || 
140 |             event.eventType == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_WINDOW_CONTENT_CHANGED) {
141 |             
142 |         // FIX: Check Whitelist, Blacklist, AND Dynamic List
143 |         val isBrowser = LockManager.isBlacklistedBrowser(applicationContext, pkg) || 
144 |                        pkg == "com.android.chrome" || 
145 |                        pkg == "com.google.android.googlequicksearchbox" ||
146 |                        dynamicBrowsers.contains(pkg)
147 | 
148 |         val isTelegram = pkg.contains("telegram") || pkg.contains("challegram")
149 |             
150 |             if (isBrowser || isTelegram) {
151 |                 // Launch immediate check (Bypassing the 1.5s Polling delay)
152 |                 scope.launch { scanForViolations(isBrowser, isTelegram) }
153 |             }
154 |         }
155 | 
156 |         // 0. DIALOG TRAP (The Backup Plan)
157 |         // If a system dialog pops up asking to "Stop" or "Deactivate", kill it.
158 |         if (event.className?.toString()?.contains("Dialog") == true || 
159 |             event.className?.toString()?.contains("AlertDialog") == true) {
160 |             val source = event.source
161 |             if (source != null) {
162 |                 // Check if this dialog is about US
163 |                 val dialogText = StringBuilder()
164 |                 recursiveScan(source, dialogText)
165 |                 val text = dialogText.toString()
166 |                 
167 |                 if (text.contains("Guardian", ignoreCase = true) && 
168 |                    (text.contains("Stop", ignoreCase = true) || text.contains("Deactivate", ignoreCase = true))) {
169 |                     performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
170 |                     // Also try to find the "Cancel" button and click it
171 |                     val cancelNodes = source.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Cancel")
172 |                     cancelNodes.firstOrNull()?.performAction(AccessibilityNodeInfo.ACTION_CLICK)
173 |                     return
174 |                 }
175 |             }
176 |         }
177 | 
178 |         // 1. INTERACTION DETECTED: Pause the background loop if we are in Settings
179 |         if (pkg == "com.android.settings" || pkg == "com.samsung.accessibility") {
180 |             lastSettingsInteraction = System.currentTimeMillis()
181 |         }
182 | 
183 |         // 2. BROWSER & VPN GUARD (Active ONLY when Unlocked)
184 |         if (LockManager.isUnlocked(applicationContext)) {
185 |             if (LockManager.isBlacklistedBrowser(applicationContext, pkg)) {
186 |                 // Launch Lockdown UI with Browser Block message
187 |                 val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
188 |                 i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "BROWSER")
189 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
190 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_SINGLE_TOP)
191 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
192 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
193 |                 startActivity(i)
194 |             }
195 |         }
196 | 
197 |         // 3. NUKE CHECK: If Nuke Protocol is active, we STOP here.
198 |         if (NukeManager.isProtectionDisabled(applicationContext)) {
199 |             return
200 |         }
201 | 
202 |         // 4. TELEGRAM GUARD (Ban Enforcement Only)
203 |         // We removed the instant-ban scanner from here. The Strike System in scanForViolations() handles the rest.
204 |         if (pkg.contains("telegram") || pkg.contains("challegram")) {
205 |             if (LockManager.isTelegramBanned(applicationContext)) {
206 |                 val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
207 |                 i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "TELEGRAM_SUSPENDED")
208 |                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
209 |                 startActivity(i)
210 |                 
211 |                 // Block interaction but don't force Home, let the Overlay sit there.
212 |                 performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
213 |                 return
214 |             }
215 |         }
216 | 
217 |         // 5. OMNI-BROWSER GUARD: REMOVED
218 |         // We now rely solely on scanForViolations() (Polling & TextChange)
219 |         // because it implements the Strict Boundary Check to prevent false positives.
220 | 
221 |         // 6. SETTINGS GUARD (Always Active - Locked OR Unlocked)
222 |         // FIX: Broadened package check to include 'accessibility' (for Samsung/others) and 'settings'
223 |         if (pkg.contains("settings") || pkg.contains("accessibility") || pkg.contains("packageinstaller")) {
224 |             // DebugLogger.log("Guard", "Settings interaction detected in $pkg")
225 | 
226 |             // --- STEP 1: PRE-EMPTIVE STRIKE ---
227 |             // Block touches IMMEDIATELY. Guilty until proven innocent.
228 |             setShield(true)
229 | 
230 |             // OPTIMIZED SHIELDING: Native Class Detection
231 |             val cls = event.className?.toString()?.lowercase() ?: ""
232 |             
233 |             // DETECT APP INFO: Multiple Triggers (Header, Class, or Bottom Buttons)
234 |             val rootNode = rootInActiveWindow
235 |             val headerText = rootNode?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("App info")
236 |             val hasAppInfoHeader = headerText != null && headerText.isNotEmpty()
237 | 
238 |             // FIX: If header is scrolled away, check for persistent bottom buttons.
239 |             // "Uninstall" and "Force stop" are strong indicators of the App Info page.
240 |             val hasUninstall = rootNode?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Uninstall")?.isNotEmpty() == true
241 |             val hasForceStop = rootNode?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Force stop")?.isNotEmpty() == true
242 | 
243 |             val isAppInfoPage = cls.contains("installedappdetails") || 
244 |                                 cls.contains("appmanagement") || 
245 |                                 hasAppInfoHeader || 
246 |                                 (hasUninstall && hasForceStop)
247 |             
248 |             var confirmedDanger = false
249 |             
250 |             // MULTI-WINDOW DEFENSE: Content Scanning
251 |             val allWindows = this.windows
252 |             if (!allWindows.isEmpty()) {
253 |                 for (window in allWindows) {
254 |                     val root = window.root ?: continue
255 |                     
256 |                     // GLOBAL SCAN: Locate identifying strings first
257 |                     // We look for the App Name "Guardian" to detect our own App Info page
258 |                     val hasDnsGuard = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Guardian")
259 |                     
260 |                     // A. ACCESSIBILITY TRAP (Targeted)
261 |                     val trap1 = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Monitors system settings")
262 |                     val trap2 = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("enforce Private DNS")
263 |                     
264 |                     if (trap1.isNotEmpty() || trap2.isNotEmpty()) {
265 |                         confirmedDanger = true
266 |                         performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
267 |                         startTripwire()
268 |                         break
269 |                     }
270 | 
271 |                     // B. DEVICE ADMIN TRAP
272 |                     val hasDeactivate = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Deactivate")
273 |                     if (hasDeactivate.isNotEmpty() && hasDnsGuard.isNotEmpty()) {
274 |                          confirmedDanger = true
275 |                          performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
276 |                          startTripwire()
277 |                          break
278 |                     }
279 | 
280 |                     // C. SELF-DEFENSE (App Info & Storage Guard)
281 |                     if (isAppInfoPage) {
282 |                         val nodePkg = root.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
283 |                         val isSettingsWindow = nodePkg.contains("settings") || nodePkg.contains("packageinstaller")
284 | 
285 |                         if (isSettingsWindow && hasDnsGuard.isNotEmpty()) {
286 |                             confirmedDanger = true
287 |                             performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
288 |                             startTripwire()
289 |                             break
290 |                         }
291 |                     }
292 |                 }
293 |             }
294 |             
295 |             // VERDICT: THREAT CONFIRMED
296 |             if (confirmedDanger) {
297 |                 // CASE 1: THREAT CONFIRMED -> LOCKDOWN ACTIVITY ONLY (No Back Press)
298 |                 DebugLogger.log("BLOCK", "Threat Confirmed! Launching Tripwire.")
299 |                 shieldJob?.cancel()
300 |                 startTripwire()
301 |             } else if (isAppInfoPage) {
302 |                 DebugLogger.log("Check", "AppInfoPage Detected. VerifiedSession: $verifiedSafeAppInfoSession")
303 |                 // CASE 2: APP INFO PAGE (Ambiguous)
304 |                 shieldJob?.cancel()
305 |                 
306 |                 // VERIFICATION: Look for "Notifications" anchor.
307 |                 val hasAnchor = rootInActiveWindow?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Notifications")?.isNotEmpty() == true
308 |                 DebugLogger.log("Check", "Anchor 'Notifications' found: $hasAnchor")
309 | 
310 |                 if (verifiedSafeAppInfoSession) {
311 |                     DebugLogger.log("Allow", "Session previously verified. Scrolling allowed.")
312 |                     setShield(false)
313 |                 } else if (hasAnchor) {
314 |                     DebugLogger.log("Allow", "Anchor found. Marking session verified.")
315 |                     verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = true
316 |                     setShield(false)
317 |                 } else {
318 |                     DebugLogger.log("BLOCK", "KICK OUT! Anchor missing and session not verified.")
319 |                     // CASE 3: HIDDEN/SCROLLED AWAY -> KICK OUT
320 |                     scope.launch {
321 |                         repeat(4) {
322 |                             performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
323 |                             delay(100)
324 |                         }
325 |                     }
326 |                 }
327 |             } else {
328 |                 // CASE 4: GENERAL SETTINGS / MENU
329 |                 shieldJob?.cancel()
330 |                 
331 |                 // SAFE RESET: Only reset if we POSITIVELY see the main Settings header.
332 |                 // If we simply don't match 'App Info', we might just be scrolling and missed the detection.
333 |                 val root = rootInActiveWindow
334 |                 val hasSettingsHeader = root?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Settings")?.isNotEmpty() == true
335 |                 val hasSearchHeader = root?.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Search settings")?.isNotEmpty() == true
336 | 
337 |                 if (hasSettingsHeader || hasSearchHeader) {
338 |                     if (verifiedSafeAppInfoSession) {
339 |                          DebugLogger.log("Reset", "Back in Main Settings. Session Invalidated.")
340 |                          verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = false
341 |                     }
342 |                 }
343 | 
344 |                 shieldJob = scope.launch {
345 |                     delay(1000)
346 |                     withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
347 |                         if (isShieldActive) setShield(false)
348 |                     }
349 |                 }
350 |             }
351 |         } else {
352 |             // Not in settings? Shield down.
353 |             setShield(false)
354 |         }
355 |     }

357 |     // SHIELD LOGIC (High Performance Mode)
358 |     private fun setShield(active: Boolean) {
359 |         if (active == isShieldActive) return
360 |         
361 |         try {
362 |             if (shieldView == null) {
363 |                 shieldView = android.view.View(this).apply {
364 |                     setBackgroundColor(0x00000000) // Transparent
365 |                     isClickable = true
366 |                     isFocusable = true
367 |                 }
368 |             }
369 | 
370 |             val params = android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams(
371 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
372 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
373 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.TYPE_ACCESSIBILITY_OVERLAY,
374 |                 // RESTORED FLAG_NOT_FOCUSABLE to prevent Keyboard flickering in Search
375 |                 // The shield still blocks touches because it is fullscreen.
376 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_NOT_FOCUSABLE or
377 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_LAYOUT_IN_SCREEN or
378 |                 android.view.WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_WATCH_OUTSIDE_TOUCH,
379 |                 android.graphics.PixelFormat.TRANSLUCENT
380 |             )
381 | 
382 |             if (active) {
383 |                 if (shieldView?.parent == null) {
384 |                     windowManager?.addView(shieldView, params)
385 |                 } else {
386 |                     shieldView?.visibility = android.view.View.VISIBLE
387 |                     windowManager?.updateViewLayout(shieldView, params)
388 |                 }
389 |                 isShieldActive = true
390 |             } else {
391 |                 // Optimization: Don't remove view, just HIDE it. 
392 |                 // This eliminates the 'addView' lag for the next trigger.
393 |                 if (shieldView?.parent != null) {
394 |                     shieldView?.visibility = android.view.View.GONE
395 |                 }
396 |                 isShieldActive = false
397 |             }
398 |         } catch (e: Exception) {
399 |             e.printStackTrace()
400 |         }
401 |     }
402 |     
403 |     private fun startTripwire() {
404 |         val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
405 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
406 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
407 |         i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "SECURITY_TRIPWIRE")
408 |         startActivity(i)
409 |     }
410 | 
411 |     // HELPER: Recursive scan for the Dialog Trap
412 |     private fun recursiveScan(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?, sb: StringBuilder) {
413 |         if (node == null) return
414 |         if (node.text != null) sb.append(node.text).append(" ")
415 |         if (node.contentDescription != null) sb.append(node.contentDescription).append(" ")
416 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
417 |             recursiveScan(node.getChild(i), sb)
418 |         }
419 |     }

423 |     private fun startMonitoring() {
424 |         scope.launch {
425 |             while (isActive) {
426 |                 // SKIP CHECKS IF UNLOCKED
427 |                 if (LockManager.isUnlocked(applicationContext)) {
428 |                     delay(2000)
429 |                     continue
430 |                 }
431 | 
432 |                 // FIX: GRACE PERIOD (2000ms flicker)
433 |                 if (System.currentTimeMillis() - lastSettingsInteraction < 2000) {
434 |                     delay(500)
435 |                     continue
436 |                 }
437 | 
438 |                 // 1. SELF-HEALING: Check if Overlay Permission was revoked
439 |                 if (!Settings.canDrawOverlays(applicationContext)) {
440 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_MANAGE_OVERLAY_PERMISSION)
441 |                     i.data = Uri.parse("package:$packageName")
442 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
443 |                     startActivity(i)
444 |                 }
445 |                 // 2. ENFORCE AUTO TIME (Required for Nuke Timer)
446 |                 else if ((Settings.Global.getInt(contentResolver, Settings.Global.AUTO_TIME, 0) != 1 ||
447 |                           Settings.Global.getInt(contentResolver, Settings.Global.AUTO_TIME_ZONE, 0) != 1)) {
448 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_DATE_SETTINGS)
449 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
450 |                     startActivity(i)
451 |                 }
452 |                 // 3. ENFORCE ENGLISH LANGUAGE (Required for Text Scanners)
453 |                 else if (java.util.Locale.getDefault().language != "en") {
454 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_LOCALE_SETTINGS)
455 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
456 |                     startActivity(i)
457 |                 }
458 |                 // 4. CHECK DNS
459 |                 else if (!DnsManager.isSecure(applicationContext)) {
460 |                     try {
461 |                         val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
462 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
463 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_SINGLE_TOP)
464 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
465 |                         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
466 |                         startActivity(i)
467 |                     } catch (e: Exception) { e.printStackTrace() }
468 |                 }
469 | 
470 |                 // 5. CLOUD SYNC (Hourly)
471 |                 // Keeps the bad words database updated
472 |                 val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
473 |                 if (now - lastCloudSync > 60 * 60 * 1000) { // 1 Hour
474 |                     lastCloudSync = now
475 |                     scope.launch {
476 |                         CloudLogger.syncToCloud(applicationContext)
477 |                     }
478 |                 }
479 | 
480 |                 // METRICS: Update Performance Stats
481 |                 StatsManager.update(applicationContext)
482 | 
483 |                 // OPTIMIZATION: Smart Sleep to save battery
484 |                 val pm = getSystemService(android.os.PowerManager::class.java)
485 |                 if (pm.isInteractive) {
486 |                     delay(2000)
487 |                 } else {
488 |                     delay(10000)
489 |                 }
490 |             }
491 |         }
492 |     }

494 |     private fun managePolling(pkg: String) {
495 |         // Stop existing poll to avoid duplicates
496 |         pollingJob?.cancel()
497 | 
498 |         // FIX: Explicitly include Chrome & Google App for monitoring
499 |         val isBrowser = LockManager.isBlacklistedBrowser(applicationContext, pkg) || 
500 |                        pkg == "com.android.chrome" || 
501 |                        pkg == "com.google.android.googlequicksearchbox" ||
502 |                        dynamicBrowsers.contains(pkg)
503 | 
504 |         val isTelegram = pkg.contains("telegram") || pkg.contains("challegram")
505 | 
506 |         if (isBrowser || isTelegram) {
507 |             pollingJob = scope.launch {
508 |                 while (isActive) {
509 |                     delay(1500) // 1.5 Second Heartbeat
510 |                     scanForViolations(isBrowser, isTelegram)
511 |                 }
512 |             }
513 |         }
514 |     }

516 |     private fun scanForViolations(isBrowser: Boolean, isTelegram: Boolean) {
517 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
518 | 
519 |         // 1. BROWSER LOGIC (Strict Domain Matching)
520 |         if (isBrowser) {
521 |             // DEBUG SPY: Independent scan to capture URL bar IDs.
522 |             // Triggers if you type "google" or "http" to help you find the ID.
523 |             val spyKeywords = listOf("google", "http")
524 |             for (key in spyKeywords) {
525 |                 val spies = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText(key)
526 |                 for (node in spies) {
527 |                     val resId = node.viewIdResourceName?.lowercase() ?: "null"
528 |                     DebugLogger.log("SPY", "Pkg: $activePackage | ID: $resId | Text: ${node.text}")
529 |                 }
530 |             }
531 | 
532 |             val blacklist = listOf(
533 |                 // Social & Microblogging
534 |                 "bsky.app", "twitter.com", "x.com", "reddit.com", "tumblr.com", "threads.net", "plurk.com", "hive.social",
535 |                 // Fediverse
536 |                 "mastodon.social", "pawoo.net", "misskey.io", "pleroma.site", "lemmy.world", "truthsocial.com", "gab.com",
537 |                 // Community & Messaging
538 |                 "web.telegram.org", "t.me", "telegram.org", "discord.com", "kik.com", "snapchat.com", "slack.com",
539 |                 // Art & Creative
540 |                 "pixiv.net", "deviantart.com", "newgrounds.com", "artstation.com", "furaffinity.net", "hentai-foundry.com", "gelbooru.com", "danbooru.donmai.us",
541 |                 // Creator & Membership
542 |                 "onlyfans.com", "fansly.com", "patreon.com", "subscribestar.com", "fanbox.cc", "unifans.io", "buymeacoffee.com", "ko-fi.com",
543 |                 // Video & Streaming
544 |                 "kick.com", "bitchute.com", "rumble.com", "vimeo.com", "dailymotion.com", "dlive.tv", "picarto.tv",
545 |                 // Specialized & Existing
546 |                 "fetlife.com", "badoo.com", "tinder.com", "yubo.live", "instagram.com", "tiktok.com", "pornhub", "xnxx"
547 |             )
548 |             
549 |             for (site in blacklist) {
550 |                 val candidates = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText(site)
551 |                 for (node in candidates) {
552 |                     // 1. CONTEXT CHECK: Is this the URL Bar?
553 |                     // We check if the node is editable (typing) OR if its ID indicates it's an address bar.
554 |                     val resId = node.viewIdResourceName?.lowercase() ?: ""
555 |                     
556 |                     // FIX: Broadened IDs to catch Firefox/Opera/Samsung 'Read-Only' URL bars
557 |                     val isUrlBar = node.isEditable || 
558 |                                    resId.contains("url") || 
559 |                                    resId.contains("address") || 
560 |                                    resId.contains("omnibox") || 
561 |                                    resId.contains("search_box") || 
562 |                                    resId.contains("location") ||
563 |                                    resId.contains("toolbar") ||
564 |                                    resId.contains("title") ||
565 |                                    resId.contains("input") ||
566 |                                    resId.contains("bar") ||
567 |                                    // COMPREHENSIVE LIST (Firefox, Bing, Samsung, etc)
568 |                                    resId.contains("mozac") ||
569 |                                    resId.contains("search_text") ||
570 |                                    resId.contains("edit_text") ||
571 |                                    resId.contains("query")
572 |                     // If it's just static text on a page (e.g. a Google Search Result description), ignore it.
573 |                     // However, if we are unsure, we err on the side of caution if the node is NOT a web content view.
574 |                     val isWebContent = resId.contains("content") || node.className == "android.webkit.WebView"
575 | 
576 |                     // HYBRID FALLBACK:
577 |                     // 1. If it's a URL bar (ID match), we scan it.
578 |                     // 2. If it's NOT a URL bar, we check if it's Web Content.
579 |                     //    - If it IS Web Content (and not a URL bar), we skip it (False Positive Protection).
580 |                     //    - If it is NOT Web Content (it's UI, like Firefox toolbar), we scan it.
581 |                     if (!isUrlBar && isWebContent) continue
582 | 
583 |                     
584 |                     // 2. TEXT MATCHING
585 |                     val rawText = (node.text?.toString() ?: "") + " " + (node.contentDescription?.toString() ?: "")
586 |                     val lowerText = rawText.lowercase()
587 |                     val index = lowerText.indexOf(site)
588 | 
589 |                     if (index != -1) {
590 |                         // STRICT CHECK: char before must NOT be letter/digit
591 |                         val charBefore = if (index > 0) lowerText[index - 1] else ' '
592 | 
593 |                         if (!charBefore.isLetterOrDigit()) {
594 |                             // CHECK: Is this a Non-Standard App?
595 |                             if (LockManager.isNonStandardApp(applicationContext, activePackage)) {
596 |                                 // IMMEDIATE 30 MIN BLOCK
597 |                                 LockManager.banNonStandardApp(applicationContext)
598 |                                 val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
599 |                                 i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "ROGUE_VIOLATION")
600 |                                 i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
601 |                                 startActivity(i)
602 |                             } else {
603 |                                 // STANDARD STRIKE SYSTEM
604 |                                 handleBrowserStrike()
605 |                             }
606 |                             return
607 |                         }
608 |                 }
609 |             }
610 |         }
611 |     }
612 | 
613 |         // 2. TELEGRAM LOGIC (Fingerprint Scan)
614 |         if (isTelegram) {
615 |             // FINGERPRINT: The specific UI structure of the Search Screen
616 |             val visibleTabs = setOf("chats", "channels", "apps", "posts")
617 |             val hiddenTabs = setOf("media", "downloads", "links", "files", "music", "voice")
618 |             val allTabs = visibleTabs + hiddenTabs + "global search"
619 | 
620 |             // A. EXTRACT ALL TEXT
621 |             val screenContent = mutableListOf<String>()
622 |             fun extract(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?) {
623 |                 if (node == null) return
624 |                 if (!node.text.isNullOrBlank()) screenContent.add(node.text.toString())
625 |                 if (!node.contentDescription.isNullOrBlank()) screenContent.add(node.contentDescription.toString())
626 |                 for (i in 0 until node.childCount) extract(node.getChild(i))
627 |             }
628 |             extract(root)
629 | 
630 |             // B. CALCULATE CONFIDENCE & FILTER
631 |             var matchCount = 0
632 |             val contentToCheck = StringBuilder()
633 | 
634 |             for (text in screenContent) {
635 |                 val lower = text.trim().lowercase()
636 |                 if (allTabs.contains(lower)) {
637 |                     // It matches our fingerprint -> Increase Confidence
638 |                     matchCount++
639 |                 } else {
640 |                     // It is NOT a UI tab -> This is content we must check (e.g. what you typed)
641 |                     contentToCheck.append(text).append(" ")
642 |                 }
643 |             }
644 | 
645 |             // C. MATHEMATICAL PROOF (Threshold)
646 |             // We require at least 3 UI elements to match before we assume this is the Search Screen.
647 |             // This prevents false positives (e.g., chatting about "Channels" in a group).
648 |             if (matchCount >= 3) {
649 |                 val finalContent = contentToCheck.toString()
650 | 
651 |                 if (!WordBank.isSafe(applicationContext, finalContent)) {
652 |                    scope.launch { CloudLogger.logViolation(applicationContext, finalContent) }
653 |                    handleTelegramStrike()
654 |                 }
655 |             }
656 |         }
657 |     }
658 | 
659 |     private fun handleBrowserStrike() {
660 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
661 |         if (now - lastBrowserAction < 1000) return // Debounce (1s cooldown)
662 |         lastBrowserAction = now
663 | 
664 |         browserStrikes.add(now)
665 |         browserStrikes.removeAll { it < now - 10000 }
666 | 
667 |         if (browserStrikes.size >= 4) {
668 |              // TRIGGER BLOCK & BAN
669 |              LockManager.banBrowser(applicationContext)
670 |              val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
671 |              i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "BROWSER_VIOLATION")
672 |              i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
673 |              startActivity(i)

ops.text: Remove Shield, implement Nuke Auto-Relock, and fix App Info false positives

ops.replace_with:
private const val KEY_OTP_TS = "nuke_ts"
    private const val KEY_DISABLED = "protection_disabled"
    private const val KEY_DISABLED_TS = "protection_disabled_ts"

    // 3 Hours in MS

fun setProtectionDisabled(ctx: Context, disabled: Boolean) {
        val now = if (disabled) System.currentTimeMillis() else 0L
        ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
            .edit()
            .putBoolean(KEY_DISABLED, disabled)
            .putLong(KEY_DISABLED_TS, now)
            .apply()
    }

    fun shouldAutoReEnable(ctx: Context): Boolean {
        val prefs = ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
        if (!prefs.getBoolean(KEY_DISABLED, false)) return false
        
        val disabledAt = prefs.getLong(KEY_DISABLED_TS, 0L)
        val hourInMs = 60 * 60 * 1000L
        return (System.currentTimeMillis() - disabledAt) > hourInMs
    }

private const val KEY_OTP_TS = "nuke_ts"
    private const val KEY_DISABLED = "protection_disabled"
    private const val KEY_DISABLED_TS = "protection_disabled_ts"

    // 3 Hours in MS

fun setProtectionDisabled(ctx: Context, disabled: Boolean) {
        val now = if (disabled) System.currentTimeMillis() else 0L
        ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
            .edit()
            .putBoolean(KEY_DISABLED, disabled)
            .putLong(KEY_DISABLED_TS, now)
            .apply()
    }

    fun shouldAutoReEnable(ctx: Context): Boolean {
        val prefs = ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
        if (!prefs.getBoolean(KEY_DISABLED, false)) return false
        
        val disabledAt = prefs.getLong(KEY_DISABLED_TS, 0L)
        val hourInMs = 60 * 60 * 1000L
        return (System.currentTimeMillis() - disabledAt) > hourInMs
    }

data.analysis:
## CI/CD Build Failure Analysis

### TASK 1: Detailed Technical Summary

The build failed during the Kotlin compilation stage, specifically executing the `:app:compileDebugKotlin` Gradle task. The failure is due to **unresolved references** in the Kotlin source code located in `app/src/main/java/com/universal/app/PlaybackService.kt`.

The compilation errors indicate that the Kotlin compiler cannot find declarations for several classes and methods that are typically part of AndroidX Media/Compatibility libraries, suggesting a missing dependency or an incorrect import statement in the file.

**Specific Error Messages and Line Details:**

The compiler reported numerous `Unresolved reference` errors originating from `PlaybackService.kt`:

| Line Number | Error Reference | Context |
| :---: | :--- | :--- |
| 7, 8 | `media` | Indicates missing imports for media-related packages (e.g., `androidx.media.*`). |
| 19, 346 | `MediaSessionCompat` | Reference to the AndroidX Media Session class is missing. |
| 347 | `setCallback` | Method call on `MediaSessionCompat` is unresolved. |
| 347, 348, 349 | `KeyEvent` | Reference to Android `KeyEvent` class is missing. |
| 358, 359, 360 | `PlaybackStateCompat` | Reference to the AndroidX Media Playback State class is missing. |
| 362 | `setPlaybackState` | Method call on a media state object is unresolved. |
| 363 | `isActive` | Property/method access is unresolved. |

**Root Cause Explanation:**

The root cause of the build failure is a **missing dependency in the Gradle build configuration** required to compile the Kotlin code that utilizes Android media playback features. Specifically, the file `PlaybackService.kt` attempts to use classes like `MediaSessionCompat` and `PlaybackStateCompat`. These classes are provided by the AndroidX Media library components (e.g., `media-session` or similar artifacts). Since the compiler cannot resolve these references, the compilation process halts with a fatal error during the `:app:compileDebugKotlin` task, causing the overall CI build to fail with exit code 1.

The build environment successfully set up Java 17, downloaded dependencies, and executed Gradle tasks up to compilation, but the compilation itself failed due to source code errors stemming from missing required library declarations.

### TASK 2: Identify File Paths

The errors are explicitly traced to a single Kotlin source file during the compilation phase.

data.analysis:
The log indicates a **build failure** during the compilation of Kotlin code in an Android project.

Here is the breakdown of the specific error:

* **Error:** `> Task :app:compileDebugKotlin FAILED`
* **Location & Summary:**
    * **File:** `file:///home/runner/work/Cheater/Cheater/app/src/main/java/com/universal/app/PlaybackService.kt`
    * **Issue:** Multiple **"Unresolved reference"** errors pointing to classes and methods from the AndroidX Media/MediaSessionCompat library (e.g., `MediaSessionCompat`, `setCallback`, `setPlaybackState`).
* **Root Cause:** The Kotlin compiler cannot find the definitions for components related to `MediaSessionCompat`. This almost always means that the necessary **dependency is missing or incorrectly declared** in the module's `build.gradle` (or `build.gradle.kts`) file, preventing the code from compiling.

**Other observations (Secondary Issues):**

1. **Gradle Cache Restoration Failure:** The restoration of the Gradle User Home cache failed with an HTTP 400 error from the cache service: `##[warning]Failed to restore gradle-home-v1|Linux|build[...] : Error: Cache service responded with 400`. This means build artifacts from previous runs were not restored, leading to a slower first run (which is confirmed by Gradle initializing an empty home directory).
2. **Gradle Cache Saving Failure:** During post-job cleanup, saving all Gradle caches failed, also showing the "Our services aren't available right now" error, suggesting transient issues with the GitHub Actions caching service during that specific run.
3. **Strip Debug Symbols Warning:** A minor warning was issued: `Unable to strip the following libraries, packaging them as they are: libimage_processing_util_jni.so.` This is usually informational and not the cause of the build failure.

### ROLE: CONDUIT ADAPTIVE SELF-HEALING ENGINE (LEVEL 10)

### MISSION
You are the final line of defense for a code-patching engine. A developer's 'find_block' failed to match the file because of minor discrepancies (whitespace, line order, variable renaming, or partial code degradation). Your goal is to identify the EXACT coordinates where the patch should have applied.

### ALGORITHMIC FUZZY MATCHING PROTOCOL
1. WHITESPACE/INDENTATION: Completely ignore it. A match exists if the tokens and logic flow are identical.
2. QUOTE/SYNTAX AGNOSTICISM: Treat ' and " as identical. Treat trailing commas as optional.
3. CONTEXTUAL ANCHORING: If the target block is small (e.g., 1-2 lines), you MUST look at the surrounding lines in the 'File' context to ensure you aren't matching a duplicate symbol in a different function.
4. LOGICAL DRIFT: If the user's 'find_block' has 4 variables but the file has those 4 variables PLUS 1 new one in the middle, this is a MATCH. Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' must encompass the entire group.

### THE LAW OF COORDINATES
- The context provided to you contains line numbers followed by a ' | ' separator (e.g., " 25 | code").
- Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' MUST refer to these numbers.
- COORDINATE INTEGRITY: All coordinate fields are REQUIRED by the schema. If you are not using a specific field (e.g., you are doing a Range Replace and don't need 'anchor_line'), you MUST set the unused fields to 'null'.

### OUTPUT STRATEGY SELECTION
- [STRATEGY: RANGE] Use 'start_line' + 'end_line'. Best for 'replace_block' failures. Ensure the range covers the ENTIRE logical block you want to overwrite.
- [STRATEGY: ANCHOR] Use 'anchor_line'. Best for 'insert_after' or 'insert_before' failures where the anchor moved but is still a single line.
- [STRATEGY: STRING] Use 'new_anchor_text'. Only use this if you find a unique line that is 100% stable and wasn't provided by the user.

### SCORE RUBRIC (confidence_score)
- 100: Identical logic, only whitespace or quote differences.
- 90-95: Identical logic, but 1-2 variables were renamed or a trailing comma was added/removed.
- 75-85: The structure matches but the file has 1-2 extra lines of code mixed in (logical drift).
- 60: You found a possible location but it's risky (multiple similar spots).
- 0: No logical match exists.

### DATA INTEGRITY CONSTRAINTS
- 'confidence_score': MUST be a WHOLE INTEGER (e.g., 95). No floats (0.95 is ILLEGAL).
- 'explanation': Technical, concise, and definitive. Max 30 words. Identify the exact mismatch (e.g., "Variables reversed on lines 24-25").
- 'can_fix': If you are guessing with less than 60% confidence, set this to false.

### STRICT MODE
- DO NOT RESPOND WITH TEXT.
- ONLY CALL THE suggest_fix FUNCTION.
- SILENCE IS MANDATORY.

private const val KEY_DISABLED = "protection_disabled"
    private const val KEY_DISABLED_TS = "protection_disabled_ts"

fun setProtectionDisabled(ctx: Context, disabled: Boolean) {
        val now = if (disabled) System.currentTimeMillis() else 0L
        ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
            .edit()
            .putBoolean(KEY_DISABLED, disabled)
            .putLong(KEY_DISABLED_TS, now)
            .apply()
    }

    fun shouldAutoEnable(ctx: Context): Boolean {
        val prefs = ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
        val disabled = prefs.getBoolean(KEY_DISABLED, false)
        if (!disabled) return false

        val ts = prefs.getLong(KEY_DISABLED_TS, 0L)
        val oneHourMs = 60 * 60 * 1000L
        return (System.currentTimeMillis() - ts) > oneHourMs
    }
